source_pr.title: executor: fix unstable fast analyze test
source_pr.body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiDB! Please read TiDB's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/pingcap/community/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document **BEFORE** filing this PR.
-->

### What problem does this PR solve? <!--add issue link with summary if exists-->

Fix https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/13498

### What is changed and how it works?

Use another way to check the histogram, only make sure that it is valid.

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

 - Unit test

Code changes

 - Has exported function/method change

Side effects

 - None

Related changes

 - None

Release note

 - None

symptoms: Fast analyze sampling produces slightly different histogram/sample outputs across runs/CI, but the test asserted an exact expected string/value.
failure_signature: Assertion failure in `executor/analyze_test.go` when comparing exact sampled histogram/sample output (e.g. `tbl.String()` or sample values) that can vary run-to-run.
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it asserted exact values/serialization of data produced by fast analyze sampling (histogram buckets / samples), which can vary depending on sampling randomness and runtime conditions. The PR stabilizes it by removing the exact string/value assertions and instead checking that the produced histogram is valid (bucket bounds are ordered and consistent).
fix_pattern: Avoid asserting exact outputs from randomized/sampling-based logic; assert invariants (valid ordering/consistency) or control the randomness/seed instead.

evidence: evidence: executor: fix unstable fast analyze test
